text:
In a small town nestled between green hills, there lived an old owl named Oliver. Despite his slight deafness, Oliver felt quite perceptive. He knew when rain was coming before it drizzled, and he could predict owlhoots' movements by the rustling in the trees.

One crisp morning, Oliver heard a curious sound under his branch. Curious, he peered down. There lay a young mouse, tangled in nets. Oliver picked her up carefully. She was scared, but Oliver smiled warmly. "Don't worry," he whispered. "I've rescued you before."

They made their way to his nest, where Oliver fixed her nets. Over time, they grew close. The mouse taught Oliver about friendship and forgiveness. Soon, there were other little creatures around—birds sharing seeds, squirrels tossing nuts. Together, they created a friendship club.

One day, a predator appeared. Owls, big and bold. But Oliver and his friends stood tall. The owl leader
